title: Volume of prisms and pyramids
description: No matter what shape the base is, if we know the height and the area of the base of prism-like or pyramid-like solids, we can find their volume.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each base in this right [[☃ definition 1]] figure is a semicircle with a radius of $7\text{ cm}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the figure?**  
*Give an exact answer in terms of pi.*

[[☃ expression 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any prism or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

###Base area

The base is a semicircle, which has half the area of a circle with the same radius.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{semicircle}}} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \pi r^2\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \pi \cdot 7^2\\\\
&=\maroonE{\dfrac{49\pi}{2}}
\end{align}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{490\pi}\text{ cm}^3$.

options.definition:
Just like a prism, except the base can be any closed 2D shape 
![A shape that is like a cylinder where the bases are semi-ellipses.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e881f0b2564778e4c92f79e0b82f68583919a9c)

options.alt: A cylinder-like figure where the bases are semicircles instead of circles. The radius of the semicircle is 7 centimeters. The height of the figure is twenty centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each base in this right [[☃ definition 1]] figure is a quarter of a circle with a radius of $2\text{ m}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the figure?**  
*Give an exact answer in terms of pi.*

[[☃ expression 1]] $\text{m}^3$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any prism or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

###Base area

The base is a quarter of a circle, which has $\dfrac14$ the area of a circle with the same radius.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{quarter circle}}} &= \dfrac{1}{4} \cdot \pi r^2\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{4} \cdot \pi \cdot 2^2\\\\
&=\maroonE{\pi}
\end{align}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{10\pi}\text{ m}^3$.

options.definition:
Just like a prism, except the base can be any closed 2D shape 
![A shape that is like a cylinder where the bases are semi-ellipses.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0e881f0b2564778e4c92f79e0b82f68583919a9c)

options.alt: A cylinder-like figure where the bases are quarters of circles instead of circles. The radius of the quarter circle is two meters. The height of the figure is ten meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
Each base in this oblique prism is a right triangle.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any prism or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

###Base area

The base is a triangle. Note that the base and height in a triangle meet at a right angle.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{triangle}}} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot bh\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 3 \cdot 4\\\\
&=\maroonE{6}
\end{align}$

###Volume

The height of a prism is the shortest distance from a point on one base to the plane of the other base. We always measure it at a right angle relative to the base. The height of this prism is $11\text{ cm}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}&=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
&=\maroonE{6} \cdot \purpleE{11}\\\\
&=\blueE{66}
\end{align}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{66}\text{ cm}^3$.

options.alt: A triangular prism with right triangles as the bases. The triangles have a hypotenuse of five centimeters, a short leg of three centimeters, and a long leg of four centimeters. The prism has a slanted length of twelve centimeters and a vertical height of eleven centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This oblique triangular prism has a volume of $63\text{ cm}^3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the base of the prism?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any prism or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

We were given the volume and height of the prism, so we can solve for the base area.

###Height

The height of a prism is the shortest distance from a point on one base to the plane of the other base. We always measure it at a right angle relative to the base. The height of this prism is $6\text{ cm}$.

###Solve for base area

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}&=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\blueE{63}&=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{6}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{63}}{\purpleE{6}} &= \maroonE{\text{base area}}\\\\
\maroonE{10.5}&= \maroonE{\text{base area}}
\end{align}$
The base area of the prism is $\maroonE{10.5}\text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: An oblique triangular prism that leans to the left at the top. The height of the figure is six centimeters. The slant height is six point three centimeters. One of the sides of the triangular bases is five centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This oblique cylinder has a volume of $90\pi\text{ m}^3$ and a base area of $12\pi\text{ m}^2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the height of the cylinder?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any prism or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

We were given the volume and base area of the cylinder, so we can solve for the height.

###Solve for height

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}&=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\blueE{90\pi}&=\maroonE{12\pi} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{90\cancel{\pi}}}{\maroonE{12\cancel{\pi}}} &= \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\purpleE{7.5}&= \purpleE{\text{height}}
\end{align}$
The height of the cylinder is $\purpleE{7.5}\text{ m}$.

options.alt: A cylinder with a slanted length. A straight dashed line moves from the circumference of one base to the center of the other base. The line is perpendicular to the base.

assessment_items.question_content:
This trapezoid-based right prism has a volume of $30\text{ cm}^3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the base of the prism?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^2$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any prism or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

We were given the volume and height of the prism, so we can solve for the base area.

###Height

The height of a prism is the shortest distance from a point on one base to the plane of the other base. We always measure it at a right angle relative to the base. 

Since the two trapezoid faces are $5\text{ cm}$ apart, the height of this prism is $\purpleE{5\text{ cm}}$.

###Solve for base area

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{prism}}}&=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\blueE{30}&=\maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{5}\\\\
\dfrac{\blueE{30}}{\purpleE{5}} &= \maroonE{\text{base area}}\\\\
\maroonE{6}&= \maroonE{\text{base area}}
\end{align}$
The base area of the prism is $\maroonE{6}\text{ cm}^2$.

options.alt: A prism with bases that are trapezoids. The height of the prism is five centimeters. The trapezoid has a larger base of six centimeters. One slanted side of the trapezoid that connects from the larger base to the smaller base of the trapezoid is one centimeter.

assessment_items.question_content:
This oblique circular cone has a radius of $5\text{ m}$ and a height of $6\text{ m}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the figure?**  
*Give an exact answer in terms of pi.*

[[☃ expression 1]] $\text{m}^3$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any pyramid or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

###Base area

The base is a circle with a radius of $5\text{ m}$.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{circle}}} &= \pi r^2\\\\
&= \pi \cdot 5^2\\\\
&=\maroonE{25\pi}
\end{align}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{50\pi}\text{ m}^3$.

options.alt: A slanted cone with a circular base radius of five meters and a height of six meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This pentagonal right pyramid has a base area of $30\text{ m}^2$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}^3$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any pyramid or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

###Base area

We were given that the base is a pentagon whose area is $30\text{ m}^2$.

$\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{pentagon}}} =\maroonE{30}$

###Volume

The height of a pyramid is the shortest distance from a point on the base to the [[☃ definition 1]]. We always measure it at a right angle relative to the base. The height of this pyramid is $\purpleE{5\text{ m}}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{30} \cdot \purpleE{5}\\\\
&=\blueE{50}
\end{align}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{50}\text{ m}^3$.

options.alt: A pentagonal right pyramid with a triangular face height of seven meters, a triangular face side of eight meters, and the pyramid's vertical height of five meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This figure shows an oblique triangle-based pyramid.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the volume of the figure?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}^3$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any pyramid or [[☃ definition 1]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

###Base area

The base is a triangle. Note that the base and height in a triangle meet at a right angle.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{triangle}}} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot bh\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 6 \cdot 8\\\\
&=\maroonE{24}
\end{align}$

###Volume

The height of a pyramid is the shortest distance from a point on the base to the [[☃ definition 1]]. We always measure it at a right angle relative to the base. The height of this pyramid is $\purpleE{11\text{ cm}}$.

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{24} \cdot \purpleE{11}\\\\
&=\blueE{88}
\end{align}$
The volume of the figure is $\blueE{88}\text{ cm}^3$.

options.alt: A triangular pyramid has a base that is a right triangle where its shortest leg of six centimeters, and a long leg of eight centimeters. One of the pyramid's faces is a right triangle where its long leg is eleven centimeters and its hypotenuse is thirty-six point two centimeters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This square-based oblique pyramid has a volume of $125\text{ m}^3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the height of the pyramid?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any pyramid or [[☃ definition 2]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

We were given the volume of the pyramid, and we can find the base area, so we can solve for the height.

###Base area

The base of the pyramid is a square with sides that are $5\text{ m}$ long.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{square}}} &= bh\\\\
&= 5 \cdot 5\\\\
&=\maroonE{25}
\end{align}$

###Solve for height

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\blueE{125}&=\dfrac{1}{3} \cdot \maroonE{25} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
3 \cdot \dfrac{\blueE{125}}{\maroonE{25}} &= \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\purpleE{15}&= \purpleE{\text{height}}
\end{align}$
The height of the pyramid is $\purpleE{15}\text{ m}$.

options.alt: A slanted pyramid with a square base. The square base has sides of five meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This trapezoid-based right pyramid has a volume of $48\text{ m}^3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the base of the pyramid?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{m}^2$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any pyramid or [[☃ definition 2]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

We were given the volume and height of the pyramid, so we can solve for the base area.

###Height

The height of a pyramid is the shortest distance from a point on the base to the [[☃ definition 1]]. We always measure it at a right angle relative to the base. The height of this pyramid is $\purpleE{8\text{ m}}$.

###Solve for base area

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\blueE{48}&=\dfrac{1}{3} \cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{8}\\\\
3 \cdot \dfrac{\blueE{48}}{\purpleE{8}} &= \maroonE{\text{base area}}\\\\
\maroonE{18}&= \maroonE{\text{base area}}
\end{align}$
The area of the base of the pyramid is $\maroonE{18}\text{ m}^2$.

options.alt: A trapezoidal pyramid with a vertical height of eight meters. Its largest triangular face is on the trapezoid's larger base side. The side of that triangle  is fourteen meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
This triangle-based oblique pyramid has a volume of $13\text{ cm}^3$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the height $h$ of the pyramid?**  

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{cm}$
hints:
###Formula

We calculate volume the same way for any pyramid or [[☃ definition 2]] solid.

$\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}$

We were given the volume of the pyramid, and we can find the base area, so we can solve for the height.

###Base area

The base is a triangle. Note that the base and height in a triangle meet at a right angle.

$\begin{align}
\maroonE{\text{area}_{\text{triangle}}} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot bh\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 3 \cdot 4\\\\
&=\maroonE{6}
\end{align}$

###Solve for height

$\begin{align}
\blueE{\text{volume}_{\text{pyramid}}}&=\dfrac{1}{3}\cdot \maroonE{\text{base area}} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\blueE{13}&=\dfrac{1}{3} \cdot \maroonE{6} \cdot \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
3 \cdot \dfrac{\blueE{13}}{\maroonE{6}} &= \purpleE{\text{height}}\\\\
\purpleE{6.5}&= \purpleE{\text{height}}
\end{align}$
The height $h$ of the pyramid is $\purpleE{6.5}\text{ cm}$.

options.alt: A slanted pyramid with a right triangular base. The base has a short leg of three centimeters. The long leg of four centimeters. The vertical height is h units. The triangular face that has its base at the short side of the right triangular base has a side of nine centimeters.
